items.front: What landing information should arriving aircraft receive as appropriate?
items.back: Issue pattern information, runway, wind, altimeter, supplements, landing clearance, requested reports, below-minima ceiling or visibility, wind shear, braking action, RwyCC, and shortened-runway information when needed.

items.front: What approach information must a nonapproach control facility forward?
items.back: Forward visual-approach clearances, arrival time, IFR cancellation, missed or overdue-aircraft information, runway in use, and required weather. If weather drops below stated minima, issue updated weather on approach.

items.front: What is the core same-runway arrival separation rule?
items.back: Do not let the arrival cross the threshold until the other aircraft is clear of the runway or the required distance minima exist. Issue required same-runway wake turbulence advisories.

items.front: When may an arrival continue on an intersecting runway or intersecting flight path?
items.back: Only after traffic information is issued and the other aircraft has passed, turned away, is clear, is holding short as required, or LAHSO protection applies. Wake intervals and cautions apply when flight paths cross.

items.front: What are the key rules for issuing landing clearance?
items.back: State the runway first, use LAHSO and runway-change phraseology when needed, address wrong-surface alignment immediately, apply LUAW restrictions, and include "shortened" whenever runway length has been reduced.

items.front: When may arrival separation be anticipated?
items.back: A landing clearance may be issued before full separation exists if observed positions show separation will exist at threshold crossing. Do not use this with LUAW except where specifically authorized.

items.front: What if an arriving aircraft should be seen but is not visually observed?
items.back: As part of the landing clearance, tell the pilot "not in sight" and restate the landing runway.

items.front: How long may landing clearance be withheld?
items.back: Do not withhold it indefinitely because an apparent violation may actually involve an emergency; assist the pilot as much as possible.

items.front: When are runway-exit or taxi instructions issued after landing?
items.back: Issue turnoff, hold short, or taxi instructions when needed for traffic or before switching to ground, protect any needed intersection, and request readback of runway hold short instructions if not received.

items.front: When may an altitude-restricted low approach be authorized?
items.back: It may be authorized at or above 500 feet over the airport, except over aircraft holding in position or departing aircraft, and not over personnel unless advised. Issue traffic or equipment location information.

items.front: How are requests for closed traffic handled?
items.back: Approve or deny them based on local traffic conditions and issue any required pattern direction or reporting instructions.

items.front: What must be issued for an overhead maneuver?
items.back: Issue pattern altitude or direction as needed, request initial and break reports, and remember the IFR flight plan is canceled at the initial point. Prevent conflicts with departures or missed approaches.

items.front: When may SFO, ELP, or practice precautionary approaches be authorized?
items.back: Only under an LOA or local procedure, with traffic information and maneuvering altitudes obtained. Request the prescribed key or final reports and issue low-approach clearance or alternate instructions there.
